chunk_summary: This section discusses the documentation and scheduling of national prophylaxis doses in clinics under India's nutrition and fortification programs, including Vitamin A supplementation (VAS), iron-folic acid (IFA) prophylaxis, and deworming initiatives.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of documenting prophylaxis doses in clinical settings.
Learn the guidelines for safe spacing of Vitamin A, IFA, and deworming doses.
Familiarize with operational guidelines from national programs like Anaemia Mukt Bharat and National Deworming Day.
Recognize the role of clinic documentation in improving compliance with national nutrition programs.
key_facts: Vitamin A supplementation (VAS) doses must be documented with the last dose and next due date.
Iron-folic acid (IFA) prophylaxis is a key component of the Anaemia Mukt Bharat initiative.
Deworming schedules are guided by the National Deworming Day operational guidelines (2015).
Clinic documentation includes therapeutic supplements prescribed and deworming last date.
The National Immunization Schedule provides a framework for prophylaxis dose timing.
reference_quote: Clinic documentation line: 'VAS last dose __; next due __; IFA prophylaxis yes/no; deworming last date __; therapeutic supplements prescribed yes/no.'
